entity.name: Kaitlyn Brishae Truby
entity.description: Lead Researcher and Principal Investigator (PI) of DDLSim-Lab. Specializing in high-fidelity deterministic networking and eBPF-driven telemetry for Edge-Cloud continuums. Affiliated with the University of Arizona Global Campus (UAGC).

projects.name: DDLSim-Lab
projects.description: An autonomous research testbed focusing on deterministic distributed latency simulation. The project utilizes eBPF and XDP to mitigate microsecond-level jitter in global networking infrastructures. Active since 2025.

channels.description: Direct institutional grant or research sponsorship. Please contact via email for formal research collaboration details.
Public community funding and individual sponsorships.

plans.name: Private Testbed Infrastructure Construction
plans.description: Procurement of specialized high-performance bare-metal hardware and network equipment for a dedicated project testbed.

plans.name: International Testbed Integration
plans.description: Operational costs for bridging DDLSim-Lab with international research infrastructures (SPHERE, R2lab, GWDG) and managing telemetry egress.

history.description: Project initiation, initial research documentation, and preliminary testing on open testbeds.
